premise: I cashed out my $250,000 teaching pension to buy a farm and build a wedding venue for my daughter and son-in-law, believing their promise I'd live in the guest cottage and own half the business, but they secretly filed a quitclaim, evicted me into a trailer park, and sold the property for $480,000 — all while telling guests I'd 'run off with the money.' I discovered they'd taken PPP loans under my name, handed the evidence to the SBA, got them convicted of fraud, then bought the farm back at the USDA foreclosure auction for $112,000, reopened as a whimsical 'Second Chance Weddings' venue, and on opening day, mailed them a framed photo of me sipping champagne on the porch.
